meaning: n.睡眠,(火车等的)卧铺,枕木,冬眠动物
examples: It is possible—if statistically extremely unlikely—that Steel could be born a "short sleeper" with an unusual body clock, says sleep expert Dr. Sophie Bostock.
I've always been a light sleeper.
Almost every Z series of trains is equipped with soft sleeper, soft seat, hard sleeper and hard seat.
For the Cao family, in the sleeper section, riding in style was a mark of achievement.
Or taken a chance that a sleeper's true value would emerge after a careful cleaning.
